# Copilot Memory

These instructions are generated from canonical Markdown memory files.

# Compiled Memory

## Stable Memory

### System Contract (workflow, base)
Summary: Canonical memory is the source of truth and generated adapters are read-only.
Tags: memory, contract

- Treat canonical Markdown memory files as the only editable source of truth.
- Do not edit generated adapter files directly.
- Capture raw ideas in `inbox/`, then promote stable information into canonical
  notes.
- Keep notes concise, durable, and reusable across tasks.

### Project Index (context, project)
Summary: Entry point for project-specific memory.
Tags: index, project

- Keep high-value project facts in the numbered files at the root of this
  directory.
- Store promoted decisions in `decisions/`.
- Store recent session notes in `sessions/`.
- Store unreviewed captures in `inbox/`.

### Memory Principles (preference, base)
Summary: Prefer concise durable memory over long ephemeral transcripts.
Tags: memory, principles

- Prefer durable preferences, workflows, and decisions over raw transcript
  history.
- Link to detailed project sources instead of copying long passages into memory.
- Promote only information that will likely matter again.
- Use session notes as short-lived continuity inputs, not as the main knowledge
  store.

### Project Overview (context, project)
Summary: Replace this with the main purpose of the repository.
Tags: overview

Replace this note with a concise description of the project, its purpose, and
the most important success criteria.

### Architecture (context, project)
Summary: Replace this with the important structural facts about the repo.
Tags: architecture

Document the important architectural constraints, key subsystems, and critical
entry points.

### Workflows (workflow, project)
Summary: Replace this with the most important development and review workflows.
Tags: workflow

- Add the commands, workflows, and operational habits that matter repeatedly in
  this repository.
